data.title: Small caps approach all-time highs, Eli Lilly at key level, 5 stocks in buy zones as Dow Jones futures surge. Ulta Beauty posts strong earnings. Robinhood leads new buys.
data.description: Almost reaching all-time highs, small capitalization stocks saw a surge as major indexes took a pause. Eli Lilly's stock price hovered around a critical level, while Robinhood dominated new purchases. Ulta Beauty’s earnings showed a strong performance in the late quarter. In other news, Dow Jones Futures indicated a positive trend in the market with small-cap stocks performing exceptionally well. Amid this, Eli Lilly's stock faced a crucial test, and five stocks entered the buy zones, showing promising potential for investors.

data.title: Small caps outperform large caps in stock market today, Nasdaq edges up, Dollar General and AppLovin break out. Dow stock hits entry.
data.description: Today, the stock market saw small cap stocks leading the way with the Nasdaq recording a slight increase, in response to a surprising decrease in weekly jobless claims. Key players like Dollar General and AppLovin also experienced significant breakout moments. The day's highlights included small caps outperforming large caps, Dollar General making a significant move forward, and a Dow stock reaching its entry level. Overall, the market demonstrated positive movement and potential for growth, with investors keeping a close eye on these developments.

data.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise stock falls after Q3 earnings report. Q4 revenue: $9.68 billion; adjusted EPS: 62 cents. Networking revenue up 150%. Server revenue down 5%. Annualized revenue run-rate increased 63% to $3.2 billion.
data.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) stock is facing selling pressure in after-hours trading due to mixed fourth-quarter financial results. The Q4 revenue stood at $9.68 billion, below the estimated $9.94 billion, with an adjusted EPS of 62 cents, exceeding the estimated 58 cents. The fiscal fourth-quarter revenue increased by 14% year-over-year, fueled by a 150% surge in networking revenue. However, server revenue saw a 5% decline, hybrid cloud revenue dropped by 12%, and financial services revenue remained unchanged. The annualized revenue run-rate of the company soared by 63% compared to the previous year, reaching $3.2 billion. Hewlett Packard Enterprise reported $2.5 billion in another financial metric.

data.title: Kinross upgraded to Baa2 by Moody’s, completes $500M senior notes redemption.
data.description: Redemption of $500 million worth of Senior Notes was successfully conducted ahead of schedule.

data.title: Dr. Pejman Ghanouni from Stanford Medicine wins RSNA’s Cum Laude Award for CAPTAIN trial on robotic radical prostatectomy, the first successful recruitment trial in its kind.
data.description: The CAPTAIN trial has achieved a milestone by becoming the initial randomized controlled study to compare a novel technology with the standard practice of robotic radical prostatectomy and successfully meeting its recruitment goal. This trial marks a significant advancement in medical research, as it aims to evaluate the effectiveness of the new technology against the current standard. The precise number of participants and the specific outcomes of the trial remain undisclosed, but this accomplishment underscores the importance of rigorous scientific investigation in improving patient care.

data.title: The PASS Coalition applauds the passage of the SAFE Sunscreen Standards Act (H.R. 3686/S. 2491) as part of the OTC Monograph User Fee Act reauthorization in H.R. 5371, improving sunscreen regulations and cancer prevention access.
data.description: In Washington, the PASS Coalition appreciates Congress for approving the SAFE Sunscreen Standards Act (H.R. 3686/S. 2491) during the recent Over-the-Counter Monograph User Fee Act (OMUFA) reauthorization in H.R. 5371. This marks a significant move in updating sunscreen ingredient authorizations and enhancing availability of secure and advanced cancer prevention resources. Outdated regulations have hindered Americans' access to effective sunscreens for years.
Fed's Bowman: We can't support RFI on future of check services. Checks remain an important payment mechanism.

data.title: WEC Energy Group to raise dividend by 6.7% to 95.25 cents per share in Q1 2026. Board of directors announced the increase.
data.description: In Milwaukee on December 4, 2025, the board of directors at WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) revealed plans to boost the quarterly dividend for their common stock to 95.25 cents per share in the first quarter of 2026. This proposed increase amounts to a 6.0% rise from the current rate.

data.title: Seritage Growth Properties repaid $20 million towards $1.6B loan from Berkshire Hathaway, totaling $1.55B since Dec. 2021.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Seritage Growth Properties (NYSE: SRG) stated that they have voluntarily made a $20 million prepayment towards their $1.6 billion term loan facility from Berkshire Hathaway Life Insurance Company of Nebraska. This brings their total repayment to $1.55 billion since December 2021, with an additional $50 million paid off. Seritage Growth Properties is a national owner and developer of retail, residential, and mixed-use properties.

data.title: Vantage Data Centers appoints Robin Balen as CPO to lead new product organization after accumulating 25 years of experience.
data.description: In Denver, Vantage Data Centers, a prominent global hyperscale data center provider, revealed the selection of Robin Balen as the chief product officer (CPO) to head the newly established product division. With over 25 years of expertise in technology and business, Balen is set to drive Vantage's worldwide product strategy and development. His role involves collaborating with various departments and regions to expedite innovation, enhance scalability, and provide top-notch solutions.

data.title: ASUR, a leading airport group, calls for shareholders' meeting in Mexico City. Trading under NYSE: ASR, BMV: ASUR.
data.description: Today, in Mexico City on December 4, 2025, Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE: ASR; BMV: ASUR) (ASUR), a prominent airport group operating in Mexico, the United States, and Colombia, revealed its General Ordinary assembly. This group is recognized internationally and oversees operations at various airports.

data.title: West Fraser Timber will indefinitely close OSB mill in High Level, Alberta in spring 2026.
data.description: A timber company based in Vancouver, British Columbia, known as West Fraser Timber Co. Ltd. (TSX and NYSE: WFG), revealed plans to shut down its oriented strand board (OSB) mill located in High Level, Alberta, by spring 2026. The closure will be permanent and conducted in an organized manner.

data.title: Brad Garlinghouse predicts Bitcoin will reach $180,000 by end of 2026, almost double its current value.
data.description: Brad Garlinghouse, the CEO of Ripple, predicts that by the end of 2026, the trading price of Bitcoin will reach $180,000. This projection suggests nearly a twofold increase from the current value.

data.title: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP urges Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) investors with substantial losses to reach out.
data.description: In Radnor, Pennsylvania, on December 4, 2025, the law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP is conducting an investigation concerning possible breaches of federal securities laws for investors of Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS). The investigation began on October 28, 2025. For more information, visit www.ktmc.com.

data.title: Kewaunee Scientific repays acquisition debt, completes successful refinancing in Statesville. (Characters: 99)
data.description: On December 4, 2025 in Statesville, North Carolina, Kewaunee Scientific Corporation (NASDAQ: KEQU), a prominent company in the field of designing, producing, and installing laboratory, healthcare, and technical furniture, shared news of its prosperous debt refinancing. The company has secured new financial agreements to address its debt and ensure stable operations moving forward.

data.title: Human Longevity increases cancer pledge to $1M to redefine colon cancer detection, prevention. Leader in precision health.
data.description: Human Longevity, Inc. (HLI), a prominent player in precision health and longevity medicine, unveiled an extension of its cancer pledge to encompass colon cancer, as part of its commitment to disease detection. The company, based in South San Francisco, Calif., stated this expansion to include the specific cancer type in their $1 million pledge aiming to further their mission in the medical field.

data.title: NatPay and Zebec partner for next-gen payroll with Web3, handling $170B annually for 300K ACH clients.
data.description: A strategic partnership has been formed between Payroll Growth Partners (PGP) of the Zebec Network and the National Payment Corporation (NatPay), a prominent ACH and document distribution provider. This collaboration aims to enhance the payroll and disbursement platform by incorporating bank-grade direct deposit infrastructure and introducing optional Web3 payout rails. NatPay, established in 1991, handles a significant volume, processing more than $170 billion annually for over 300,000 ACH clients. This aligns NatPay as one of the largest providers in the U.S. for such services.

data.title: Dell Technologies declares $0.525 quarterly cash dividend per share, up 18% annually to $2.10/share. Payable on Jan. 30, 2026.
data.description: Dell Technologies, a leading technology company based in Round Rock, Texas, recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.525 per common share. The dividend is set to be paid on January 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 20, 2026. This decision follows an 18% increase in the annual cash dividend to $2.10 per common share, approved by the board in February. Dell Technologies, traded on the NYSE under the symbol DELL, focuses on assisting organizations and individuals in shaping their digital futures.

data.title: Canoe EIT Income Fund to distribute $0.10 per unit in Dec 2025. Record date: Dec 22, 2025; payment date: Jan 15, 2026.
data.description: In Calgary, Alberta, on December 4, 2025, Canoe EIT Income Fund (TSX - EIT.UN) disclosed a monthly distribution for December 2025 at $0.10 per unit. Those recorded as unitholders by December 22, 2025, are set to receive payouts on January 15, 2026.
Federal Reserve Board requests public input on the impact of potential strategic changes to check services provided by the Fed, as well as check usage and preferences
